Code: 65/2/1 (set-1)

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the product of the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(x y^2\right)\right] \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0.

 2
2.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sin 3 x}{\sin x} d x{/tex}

 2

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{1}{2} \log 3} \frac{e^x}{e^{2 x}+1} d x{/tex}

 2
3.

{tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two unit vectors such that {tex}|2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}|=|3 \vec{a}-2 \vec{b}|{/tex}. Find the angle between {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

 2
4.

A pair of dice is thrown. It is given that the sum of numbers appearing on both dice is an even number. Find the probability that the number appearing on at least one die is 3.

 2
5.

Probabilities of A and B solving a specific problem are {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}, respectively. If both of them try independently to solve the problem, then find the probability that the problem is solved.

 2
6.

Write the cartesian equation of the line PQ passing through points P(2, 2, 1) and Q(5, 1, -2). Hence, find the y-coordinate of the point on the line PQ whose z-coordinate is -2.

 2

SECTION B

Questions number 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

ABCD is a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. Find {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex}. Also, find the area of the parallelogram ABCD.

 3
8.(i)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^1 \tan ^{-1} x \ d x{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex}

 3
9.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}{/tex} = x, given that y = 1, when x = 1.

 3
10.(i)

Find the equation of the plane passing through points (2, 1, 0), (3, -2, -2) and (1, 1, -7). Also, obtain its distance from the origin.

 3

OR
(ii)

Find the distance between the lines x = {tex}\frac{y-1}{2}=\frac{z-2}{3}{/tex} and x + 1 = {tex}\frac{y+2}{2}=\frac{z-1}{3}{/tex}.

 3

SECTION C

Questions number 11 to 14 carry 4 marks each.
11.

Find the distance between the point (-1, -5, -10) and the point of intersection of the line {tex}\frac { x - 2 } { 3 } = \frac { y + 1 } { 4 } = \frac { z - 2 } { 12 }{/tex}and the plane x - y + z = 5.

 4
12.

Evaluate {tex}\int_{0}^{1} x(1-x)^{n} d x{/tex} 

 4
13.(i)

Using integration, find the area of the smaller region enclosed by the curve 4x2 + 4y2 = 9 and the line 2x + 2y = 3.

 4

OR
(ii)

If the area of the region bounded by the curve y2 = 4ax and the line x = 4a is {tex}\frac{256}{3}{/tex} sq. units, then using integration, find the value of a, where a > 0.

 4

Case-Study Based Question
14.

At the start of a cricket match, a coin is tossed and the team winning the toss has the opportunity to choose to bat or bowl. Such a coin is unbiased with equal probabilities of getting head and tail.

Based on the above information, answer the following questions:

(i)

If such a coin is tossed 2 times, then find the probability distribution of number of tails.

 2
(ii)

Find the probability of getting at least one head in three tosses of such a coin.

 2

Annual Exam - 2022

Code: 65/2/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 7 printed pages.
  • Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 14 questions.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:2 hours
Maximum Marks:40

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them :

  1. The question paper contains three sections - Section A, B and C.
  2. Each section is compulsory.
  3. Section A has 6 short answer type I questions of 2 marks each.
  4. Section B has 4 short answer type II questions of 3 marks each.
  5. Section C has 4 long answer type questions of 4 marks each.
  6. There is an internal choice in some questions.
  7. Question no. 14 is a case-study based question with 2 sub-parts of 2 marks each.

SECTION A

Questions number 1 to 6 carry 2 marks each.
1.

Find the product of the order and the degree of the differential equation {tex}\left[\frac{d}{d x}\left(x y^2\right)\right] \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}+y{/tex} = 0.

Solution:

{tex}\left[\frac d{dx}\left(xy^2\right)\right].\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\frac d{dx}\left(uv\right)=u\frac{dv}{dx}+v\frac{du}{dx}{/tex}
So using
{tex}\left(x\frac d{dx}y^2+y^2\frac{dx}{dx}\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\left(2xy\frac{dy}{dx}+y^2\right)\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}2xy\left(\frac{dy}{dx}\right)^2+y^2\frac{dy}{dx}+y=0{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} order = 1
Degree = 2
Product of order and degree = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} 2 = 2

 2
2.(i)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{\sin 3 x}{\sin x} d x{/tex}

Solution:

‚ąĶ sin 3x = 3 sinx - 4sin3x
{tex}\int{\textstyle\frac{\sin3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}=\int{\textstyle\frac{3\sin x-4\sin^3x}{\sin x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\sin^2xdx{/tex}
Putting {tex}sin^2x=\frac{1-cos2x}2{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-4\int\;\frac{1-\cos2x}2dx{/tex}
{tex}=3\int dx-\frac42\int\;dx+\frac42\int\cos2x\;dx{/tex}
= 3x - 2x + 2 {tex}\frac{sin2x}2{/tex} +C {tex}\left\{\int\cos\;ax=\frac{\sin\;ax}a\right\}{/tex}
= x + sin2x +C

 2

OR
(ii)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^{\frac{1}{2} \log 3} \frac{e^x}{e^{2 x}+1} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+e^{2x}}\;dx{/tex}
{tex}\int_0^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}\frac{e^x}{1+\left(e^x\right)^2}\;dx{/tex}
Put ex = t, exdx=dt
Changing limit
at x = 0, t = e0 = 1
at x = {tex}\frac12\log3{/tex}, t = {tex}e^{{\textstyle\frac12}\log3}=e^{\log\sqrt3}=\sqrt3{/tex}
So,
{tex}\int_1^\sqrt3\frac{dt}{1+t^2}=\left[\tan^{-1}t\right]_1^\sqrt3{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\tan^{-1}\sqrt3-\tan^{-1}1\right]=\frac\pi3-\frac\pi4{/tex}
{tex}=\frac\pi{12}{/tex}

 2
3.

{tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex} are two unit vectors such that {tex}|2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}|=|3 \vec{a}-2 \vec{b}|{/tex}. Find the angle between {tex}\vec{\mathrm{a}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{b}}{/tex}.

Solution:

Let {tex}\left|\overrightarrow a\right|=\left|\overrightarrow b\right|=1{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}
{tex}\left|2\overrightarrow a+3\overrightarrow b\right|^2=\left|3\overrightarrow a-2\overrightarrow b\right|^2{/tex}
{tex}4\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+9\left|\overrightarrow b\right|^2+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|=9\left|\overrightarrow a\right|^2+4\left|\overrightarrow b\right|-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex}
{tex}4\times1+9\times1+12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}=9\times1+4\times1-12\left|\overrightarrow a.\overrightarrow b\right|{/tex} {tex}24\left|\overrightarrow a\right|\left|\overrightarrow b\right|\cos\theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta=0{/tex}
{tex}\cos\theta=\cos0^\circ{/tex}
{tex}\theta=0^\circ{/tex}

 2
4.

A pair of dice is thrown. It is given that the sum of numbers appearing on both dice is an even number. Find the probability that the number appearing on at least one die is 3.

Solution:

A pair of dice is thrown.
Total outcomes, n = 36
Let A denote the event that the sum of numbers appearing on both dice is an even number and let B denote the event that the number appearing on at least one die is 3.
Now, Number of favourable outcomes for A, n(A) = {(1, 1), (1, 3), (1, 5), (2, 2), (2, 4), (2, 6), (3, 1), (3, 3), (3, 5), (4, 2), (4, 4), (4, 6), (5, 1), (5, 3), (5, 5), (6, 2), (6, 4), (6, 6)} = 18
Number of favourable outcomes for B, n(B) = {(1, 3), (2, 3), (3, 3), (4, 3), (5, 3), (6, 3), (3, 1), (3, 2), (3, 4), (3, 5), (3, 6)} = 11
Number of favorable outcomes for A∩B, n(A∩B) = {(1, 3), (3, 3), (5, 3), (3, 1), (3, 5)} = 5
P (A) = {tex}\frac{n(A)}n=\frac{18}{36}=\frac12{/tex}
P (B) = {tex}\frac{n(B)}n=\frac{11}{36}{/tex}
{tex}P(A\cap B)=\frac{n(A\cap B)}n=\frac5{36}{/tex}
Now,
P ({tex}\frac AB{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{P(A\cap B)}{P(B)}{/tex}
P ({tex}\frac AB{/tex}) = {tex}\frac{\frac5{36}}{\frac{11}{36}}=\frac5{11}{/tex}

 2
5.

Probabilities of A and B solving a specific problem are {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}, respectively. If both of them try independently to solve the problem, then find the probability that the problem is solved.

Solution:

{tex}\because{/tex} P(A) = {tex}\frac23{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äčand P(B) = {tex}\frac35{/tex}
{tex}P(A\cap B)=P(A).P(B){/tex}
{tex}P(A\cap B)=\frac23\times\frac35=\frac25{/tex}
P (problem is solved) = Probability that A solves the problem or B solves the problem
P (Problem solved) = {tex}P(A\cup B){/tex}
= {tex}P(A)+P(B)-P(A\cap B){/tex}‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč= {tex}\frac23+\frac35-\frac25{/tex}‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč= {tex}\frac{10+9+6}{15}=\frac{25}{15}{/tex}
P (Problem solved) = {tex}\frac53{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

 2
6.

Write the cartesian equation of the line PQ passing through points P(2, 2, 1) and Q(5, 1, -2). Hence, find the y-coordinate of the point on the line PQ whose z-coordinate is -2.

Solution:

P (2, 2, 1) and Q(5, 1, -2)
From equation of line passing through two points
{tex}\frac{x-x_1}{x_2-x_1}=\frac{y-y_1}{y_2-y_1}=\frac{z-z_1}{z_2-z_1}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{x-2}{5-2}=\frac{y-2}{1-2}=\frac{z-1}{-2-1}{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} it is given that z = -2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{x-2}{5-2}=\frac{y-2}{1-2}=\frac{-2-1}{-2-1}{/tex}
On comparing
{tex}\frac{y-2}{-1}=1{/tex}
y - 2 = -1
y = 1

 2

SECTION B

Questions number 7 to 10 carry 3 marks each.
7.

ABCD is a parallelogram such that {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}. Find {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}}{/tex} and {tex}\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}}{/tex}. Also, find the area of the parallelogram ABCD.

Solution:


{tex}\because{/tex} {tex}\vec{\mathrm{AC}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}{/tex} and {tex}\vec{\mathrm{BD}}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
Let {tex}\vec{AB}=\;\vec a{/tex} and {tex}\vec{BC}=\vec b{/tex}
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC,
using vector addition law in triangle
{tex}\;\vec a+\vec b=\vec{AC}{/tex} ...(1)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
and in {tex}\triangle{/tex}DBA,
{tex}\;\vec{BD}=-\vec a+\vec b=\vec b-\vec a{/tex}...(2)
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčAdding eqn (1) and (2)
{tex}2\vec b=\hat i+\hat j+2\hat i+\hat j+\hat k{/tex} = {tex}3\hat i+2\hat j+\hat k{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč {tex}\vec b=\frac32\hat i+\hat j+\frac12\hat k{/tex}
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčNow, subtracting eqn (2) from (1)
{tex}2\vec a=\vec{AC}-\vec{BD}=-\hat i-\hat k{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč {tex}\vec a=-\frac12\hat i-\frac12\hat k{/tex}‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Now, the area of ABCD = {tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|{/tex}
{tex}\frac12\left|\vec{d_1}\times\vec{d_2}\right|=\frac12\begin{vmatrix}i&j&k\\1&1&0\\2&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
{tex}=\frac12\left|\left[i\left(1-0\right)-j\left(1-0\right)+k\left(1-2\right)\right]\right|{/tex}‚Äč
‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč {tex}=\frac12\left|i-j-k\right|{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
{tex}=\frac12\sqrt{1+1+1}=\frac{\sqrt3}2{/tex}sq.unit‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

 3
8.(i)

Evaluate: {tex}\int \limits_0^1 \tan ^{-1} x \ d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \limits_0^1 \tan ^{-1} x \ d x{/tex}
Using integration by parts
Taking u = {tex}\tan^{-1}x{/tex}, v = 1
{tex}I=\tan^{-1}x\int1.dx-\int{\textstyle\frac1{1+x^2}}\left(\int1.dx\right)dx{/tex}
{tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-\int{\textstyle\frac x{1+x^2}}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\int{\textstyle\frac{2x}{1+x^2}}dx{/tex}
{tex}I=\left[x\tan^{-1}x-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+x^2\right|\right]_0^1{/tex}
{tex}I=1\tan^{-1}1-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|1+1\right|-0+{\textstyle\frac12}\log1{/tex}
{tex}I=\frac\pi4-{\textstyle\frac12}\log\left|2\right|{/tex}

 3

OR
(ii)

Find: {tex}\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex}

Solution:

{tex}I=\int \frac{2 x}{x^2+3 x+2} d x{/tex}
{tex}I=\int\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}dx{/tex}
Using partial fraction
{tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac A{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac B{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
2x = Ax + 2A + Bx + B
2x = (A + B)x + 2A + B
On compairing
A + B = 2, 2A + B = 0
A = -2 , B = 4
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{2x}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\frac{-2}{\left(x+1\right)}+\frac4{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\int\frac{2xdx}{\left(x+1\right)\left(x+2\right)}=\int\frac{-2dx}{\left(x+1\right)}+\int\frac{4dx}{\left(x+2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}I=-2\log\left|x+1\right|+4\log\left|x+2\right|+C{/tex}

 3
9.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation {tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}{/tex} = x, given that y = 1, when x = 1.

Solution:

{tex}\left(y+3 x^2\right) \frac{d x}{d y}=x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dx}{dy}=\frac x{\left(y+3x^2\right)}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac{y+3x^2}x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}=\frac yx+3x{/tex}
{tex}\frac{dy}{dx}-\frac1xy=3x{/tex}
It is in the form of a linear differential equation
∴ P = {tex}\frac{-1}x{/tex}, Q = 3x
I.F. = {tex}e^{-\int\frac1xdx}=e^{-\log x}=e^{\log x^{-1}}=\frac1x{/tex}
∴ {tex}\frac yx=\int\frac1x.3x\;dx=3\int dx{/tex}
{tex}\frac{y}x{/tex} = 3x + C
y = 3x² + Cx ...(i)
Now at x = 1, y = 1
So, 1 = 3 + C
C = -2
Putting in eqn (i)
y = 3x2 - 2x

 3
10.(i)

Find the equation of the plane passing through points (2, 1, 0), (3, -2, -2) and (1, 1, -7). Also, obtain its distance from the origin.

Solution:

We know that, the equation of a plane passing through three non-collinear points
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}x-x_1&y-y_1&z-z_1\\x_2-x_1&y_2-y_1&z_2-z_1\\x_3-x_1&y_3-y_1&z_3-z_1\end{vmatrix}=0{/tex}
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}x-2&y-1&z-0\\3-2&\left(-2\right)-1&\left(-2\right)-0\\1-2&1-1&\left(-7\right)-0\end{vmatrix}=0{/tex}
{tex}\begin{vmatrix}x-2&y-1&z-0\\1&-3&-2\\-1&0&-7\end{vmatrix}=0{/tex}‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
(x - 2)(21) - (y - 1)(-7 - 2) + z(0 - 3) = 0
21x - 42 + 9y - 9 - 3z = 0
21x + 9y - 3z = 51
